gists.title: Agentic Flow 1.6.4 + QUIC
gists.author: ruvnet
gists.purpose: CLI framework for deploying AI agents over QUIC transport protocol with distributed reasoning fabric
key_features: QUIC Protocol Foundation (RFC 9000) with TLS 1.3 encryption
Connection migration for seamless network switching
Per-stream flow control and independent failure isolation
Global Synaptic Fabric for community-shared routing
Intent Channels for purpose tokens vs content separation

architecture_patterns.distributed_cognition: Each QUIC stream as independent cognitive thread
architecture_patterns.cost_optimization: Model selection routing with quality thresholds

use_cases.name: Refactoring Pipeline
use_cases.description: Static analysis, type checking, transforms, tests concurrent

applicable_areas: Parallel test execution across distributed agents
Resilient agent connections during long test runs
Cost optimization for LLM-powered test generation

expected_benefits.reliability: Connection migration for long test suites
expected_benefits.throughput: Massive improvement in test result streaming

risks: UDP firewall restrictions in enterprise environments
Added complexity for debugging network issues

gists.title: AgentDB
gists.author: ruvnet
gists.purpose: Sub-millisecond memory engine for autonomous agents with persistent cognitive infrastructure
key_features: HNSW algorithm for 12-116x faster vector search
Batch operations: 144-676x faster than individual inserts

reasoningbank_components.PatternMatcher: Stores and retrieves task execution patterns with success metrics
reasoningbank_components.ExperienceCurator: Tracks performance experiences with quality scoring
reasoningbank_components.MemoryOptimizer: Collapses historical data (85% reduction) for long-term storage
reasoningbank_components.ContextSynthesizer: Aggregates weighted patterns, experiences, and memories

capabilities: Vector operations (insert, batch, search, delete, stats)

applicable_areas: Test result embeddings for similarity search
Test data template storage and retrieval
Regression risk analysis with historical data

expected_benefits.intelligence: Self-improving test generation through pattern learning
expected_benefits.efficiency: 85% memory reduction for long-term test history
expected_benefits.cost: Local-first architecture eliminates external DB costs

risks: SQLite limitations for massive distributed deployments
QUIC sync complexity for multi-agent scenarios

synergies.benefit: Ultra-fast agent coordination with persistent learning
synergies.use_case: Distributed test agents sharing learned patterns via QUIC streams

synergies.benefit: Cost-optimized LLM selection based on learned patterns
synergies.use_case: Automatically route simple tests to GPT-3.5, complex to Claude based on success history

synergies.benefit: Parallel test execution with real-time pattern matching
synergies.use_case: 100+ test agents searching similar test cases concurrently

synergies.benefit: Resilient long-running tests with stateful learning
synergies.use_case: Mobile API testing maintaining state across network changes

complementary_features.integrated_value: Complete intelligent testing platform with transport, memory, and execution

tasks: Install and configure AgentDB with better-sqlite3
Integrate MCP server for agent memory access
Implement test pattern storage using PatternMatcher
Create vector embeddings for test cases and results
Build basic similarity search for test reuse
expected_outcomes: Agents can store and retrieve test patterns
Test generation improves through pattern learning
Duplicate test detection via similarity search

tasks: Integrate ExperienceCurator for test quality scoring
Implement MemoryOptimizer for long-term test history
Deploy ContextSynthesizer for intelligent test selection
Create Q-Learning plugin for test optimization
Build performance metric tracking in memory
Integrate QUIC protocol library
Implement agent routing over QUIC streams
Build multi-provider LLM routing with cost optimization
Create connection migration for long test runs
Setup stream multiplexing for parallel execution
Implement QUIC-based AgentDB synchronization
Build delta-based memory sync between agents
Create conflict resolution strategies
Deploy mesh topology for peer-to-peer learning
Setup global pattern sharing across fleet
expected_outcomes: Distributed test agents share learned patterns

implementation_recommendations.reason: Lowest complexity, highest immediate value, no infrastructure changes
Higher complexity, requires infrastructure changes, validate need first
validate_first: UDP firewall restrictions in target environments

agentdb: SQLite scaling limits (consider PostgreSQL + pgvector migration path)
Vector embedding quality dependency (need robust embedding strategy)
Backup and recovery procedures for learned patterns
agentic_flow: Firewall and network policy updates (enterprise compatibility)

quic_integration.cost_savings: 85-98% reduction in LLM costs (validated in production)

agentdb_risks.sqlite_limits: Start with PostgreSQL + pgvector as primary, AgentDB for local dev
agentdb_risks.data_loss: Implement automated backup to object storage (S3/GCS)

quic_risks.firewall_restrictions: Provide HTTP/2 fallback mode for enterprise environments
